SimpleCoder: 
import argparse
import eyed3

def view_tags(file_path):
    audiofile = eyed3.load(file_path)
    print(f"Title: {audiofile.tag.title}")
    print(f"Artist: {audiofile.tag.artist}")
    print(f"Album: {audiofile.tag.album}")
    print(f"Year: {audiofile.tag.recording_date}")

def edit_tags(file_path, title=None, artist=None, album=None, year=None):
    audiofile = eyed3.load(file_path)
    if title:
        audiofile.tag.title = title
    if artist:
        audiofile.tag.artist = artist
    if album:
        audiofile.tag.album = album
    if year:
        audiofile.tag.recording_date = year
    audiofile.tag.save()

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="MP3 Tag Editor")
    parser.add_argument("file_path", help="Path to the MP3 file")
    parser.add_argument("--view", action="store_true", help="View the tags of the MP3 file")
    parser.add_argument("--title", help="Title of the song")
    parser.add_argument("--artist", help="Artist of the song")
    parser.add_argument("--album", help="Album of the song")
    parser.add_argument("--year", help="Year of the song")

    args = parser.parse_args()

    if args.view:
        view_tags(args.file_path)
    else:
        edit_tags(args.file_path, args.title, args.artist, args.album, args.year)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import pytest
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock

# Assuming the functions are in a module named mp3_tag_editor.py
from mp3_tag_editor import view_tags, edit_tags

@patch("mp3_tag_editor.eyed3.load")
def test_view_tags(mock_load):
    mock_audiofile = MagicMock()
    mock_audiofile.tag.title = "Test Title"
    mock_audiofile.tag.artist = "Test Artist"
    mock_audiofile.tag.album = "Test Album"
    mock_audiofile.tag.recording_date = "2023"
    mock_load.return_value = mock_audiofile

    with patch("builtins.print") as mock_print:
        view_tags("dummy_path.mp3")
        mock_print.assert_any_call("Title: Test Title")
        mock_print.assert_any_call("Artist: Test Artist")
        mock_print.assert_any_call("Album: Test Album")
        mock_print.assert_any_call("Year: 2023")

@patch("mp3_tag_editor.eyed3.load")
def test_edit_tags_title(mock_load):
    mock_audiofile = MagicMock()
    mock_load.return_value = mock_audiofile

    edit_tags("dummy_path.mp3", title="New Title")
    mock_audiofile.tag.save.assert_called_once()
    assert mock_audiofile.tag.title == "New Title"

def test_edit_tags_artist(mock_load):

    edit_tags("dummy_path.mp3", artist="New Artist")

import pytest
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock
from mp3_tag_editor import view_tags, edit_tags

def test_view_tags_no_tags():
    with patch("mp3_tag_editor.eyed3.load") as mock_load:
        mock_audiofile = MagicMock()
        mock_audiofile.tag.title = None
        mock_audiofile.tag.artist = None
        mock_audiofile.tag.album = None
        mock_audiofile.tag.recording_date = None
        mock_load.return_value = mock_audiofile

        with patch("builtins.print") as mock_print:
            view_tags("empty_tags.mp3")
            mock_print.assert_any_call("Title: None")
            mock_print.assert_any_call("Artist: None")
            mock_print.assert_any_call("Album: None")
            mock_print.assert_any_call("Year: None")

@patch("mp3_tag_editor.eyed3.load")
def test_edit_tags_all(mock_load):
    mock_audiofile = MagicMock()
    mock_load.return_value = mock_audiofile

    edit_tags("dummy_path.mp3", title="Full Title", artist="Full Artist", album="Full Album", year="2025")
    mock_audiofile.tag.save.assert_called_once()
    assert mock_audiofile.tag.title == "Full Title"
    assert mock_audiofile.tag.artist == "Full Artist"
    assert mock_audiofile.tag.album == "Full Album"
    assert mock_audiofile.tag.recording_date == "2025"

@patch("mp3_tag_editor.eyed3.load")
def test_edit_tags_none(mock_load):
    mock_audiofile = MagicMock()
    mock_load.return_value = mock_audiofile

    edit_tags("dummy_path.mp3")
    mock_audiofile.tag.save.assert_not_called()

@patch("mp3_tag_editor.eyed3.load")
def test_edit_tags_with_invalid_year(mock_load):
    mock_audiofile = MagicMock()
    mock_load.return_value = mock_audiofile

    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        edit_tags("dummy_path.mp3", year="invalid_year")

@patch("mp3_tag_editor.eyed3.load")
def test_view_tags_with_nonexistent_file(mock_load):
    mock_load.side_effect = FileNotFoundError

    with pytest.raises(FileNotFoundError):
        view_tags("nonexistent_file.mp3")

SimpleReviewer: One critical comment on the test cases provided, particularly regarding the handling of invalid input and error scenarios, is the absence of tests that specifically address how the application behaves when encountering invalid or malformed MP3 files. While there is a test case (`test_view_tags_with_nonexistent_file`) that checks the application's behavior when a file does not exist, there is no test case that evaluates the application's resilience and error handling when dealing with MP3 files that are corrupt, partially downloaded, or not MP3 files at all (e.g., a different file format mistakenly given an MP3 extension).

This gap in testing is significant because handling such scenarios gracefully is crucial for the robustness of the application. Users might inadvertently attempt to view or edit tags on files that are not valid MP3 files, and the application should provide clear, informative error messages rather than crashing or behaving unpredictably. Testing for these scenarios would ensure that the application can handle a wide range of real-world use cases and error conditions effectively.
